name: From Castillo de Montizón to Torre de la Higuera — circular from Villamanrique
name_original: Del Castillo de Montizón por la Torre de la Higuera — circular desde Villamanrique
region_intro: Hiking around Campo De Montiel offers diverse landscapes, from dry plains and rolling hills to small mountains. The region is characterized by a mosaic of continental Mediterranean environments, including extensive holm oak forests, scrubland, and significant wetlands. Notably, the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park features a chain of lagoons with clear, turquoise waters, providing unique natural settings for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Campo De Montiel The most popular hiking route is Villanueva de los Infantes - Fuenllana Circular Trail, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route allows exploration of plains and historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Quijote by the Peñarroya Reservoir — circular through the Natural Park Lagunas de Ruidera, a difficult 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This trail explores the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park, known for its clear waters and ecological importance. Local hikers also love the From Castillo de Montizón to Torre de la Higuera — circular from Villamanrique, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around Campo De Montiel is defined by dry plains, rolling hills, and the distinctive lagoons of the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Campo De Montiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Campo De Montiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Campo de Montiel?
A: Campo de Montiel offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 25 routes available. These trails cater to different skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Campo de Montiel?
A: Hikers in Campo de Montiel will encounter diverse landscapes, from dry plains and gently rolling hills to small mountains. The region is characterized by Mediterranean scrubland, extensive holm oak forests, olive groves, and significant wetlands, particularly around the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park. You can expect a mix of natural beauty and historical settings.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Campo de Montiel?
A: Yes, Campo de Montiel has several easy trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200989788/from-montiel-to-the-alameda-de-montiel-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Montiel to the Alameda de Montiel — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that allows you to explore the local area without significant elevation changes. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200944055/ascent-to-the-castle-of-montiel-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castle of Montiel — circular&lt;/a&gt;, offering historical views.

Q: Can I find challenging or difficult hikes in Campo de Montiel?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Campo de Montiel offers several difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199577968/route-of-the-quijote-by-the-penarroya-reservoir-circular-through-the-natural-park-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Quijote by the Peñarroya Reservoir — circular through the Natural Park Lagunas de Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, which is a demanding 12.5-mile path exploring the stunning Ruidera Lakes Natural Park. Another challenging trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209068941/from-castillo-de-montizon-to-torre-de-la-higuera-circular-from-villamanrique" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Castillo de Montizón to Torre de la Higuera — circular from Villamanrique&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 13 miles through historical landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Campo de Montiel?
A: Yes, many trails in Campo de Montiel are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198578324/villanueva-de-los-infantes-fuenllana-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de los Infantes - Fuenllana Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which combines plains and historical sites, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209099931/the-olive-fields-of-almedina-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Olive Fields of Almedina — circular&lt;/a&gt;, showcasing the region's agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that feature the Ruidera Lakes?
A: The Ruidera Lakes Natural Park is a highlight of Campo de Montiel, and several trails explore this beautiful area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199577968/route-of-the-quijote-by-the-penarroya-reservoir-circular-through-the-natural-park-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Quijote by the Peñarroya Reservoir — circular through the Natural Park Lagunas de Ruidera&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering stunning views of the clear, turquoise waters. You can also visit specific points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5598095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca&lt;/a&gt; within the park.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore while hiking in Campo de Montiel?
A: Campo de Montiel is rich in history, with many trails passing by significant landmarks. You can hike to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209173765/castillo-de-albaladejo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Albaladejo&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209068941/from-castillo-de-montizon-to-torre-de-la-higuera-circular-from-villamanrique" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montizón&lt;/a&gt; and the Torreón de la Higuera. The region also features historical towns like Villanueva de los Infantes, a National Historic-Artistic Site, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of Santo Domingo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the trails in Campo de Montiel?
A: Yes, the region is home to natural features including waterfalls. Hikers can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5884091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hundimiento Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, located within the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park area, which is a popular attraction for its scenic beauty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Campo de Montiel?
A: The hiking routes in Campo de Montiel are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites, and the clear waters of the Ruidera Lakes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Campo de Montiel?
A: The continental Mediterranean climate of Campo de Montiel makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant, with lush vegetation or beautiful autumn colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Campo de Montiel suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Campo de Montiel are suitable for families, especially the easier and moderate routes. Look for shorter, less strenuous paths that offer interesting natural features or historical points to keep children engaged. The interpretive trails around the Ruidera Lakes, such as the Ossero Trail, are often good choices for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Campo de Montiel?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Campo de Montiel. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks like Ruidera Lakes, to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Be sure to check specific regulations for any protected areas you plan to visit.
tips: As you pass along this road and on the right you can see on the nearest hill what is called Torreón de la Higuera, with the predominant tower above the rest.

Montizón Castle is a historic fortification located in the municipality of Villamanrique, in the province of Ciudad Real. Built in the 13th century, this castle belonged to the Order of Santiago and was the residence of the poet Jorge Manrique.
The castle is located in a strategic position above the Guadalén River, taking advantage of a natural cut in the hill where it is located. The keep, one of its most outstanding structures, is built on solid rock and offers impressive views of the surroundings.
Currently, the castle is privately owned and can be visited at specific times, generally on weekends and by appointment.

waypoints.name: Views of Torre de la Higuera
